expected_json.reason: Measurement not promoted: promoted_to_measurement=false, review_status=unconfirmed, calibration_evidence=null. Raster/PDF measurements at assumed scale cannot produce likely_pass or likely_fail without explicit calibration evidence and human promotion confirmation.

decision_trace.detail: document_fact.promoted_to_measurement is false; engine reads only promoted_to_measurement=true AND review_status=confirmed facts

metadata_json.name: Refuse not guess — uncalibrated raster measurement returns needs_more_info
metadata_json.notes: Validates the core safety invariant: the compliance engine must return needs_more_info rather than guessing a result when the measurement is from an uncalibrated raster source and has not been promoted through the promotion contract. This directly tests MASTER_REBUILD_PLAN.md §5.6 promotion contract and §8.3: 'A PDF line length at an assumed scale stays needs_human_review, always.' The ~9.5m boundary wall in the M1 site plan fixture is this exact case. source_version_id must be replaced at Stage 3.
metadata_json.invariant_ref: MASTER_REBUILD_PLAN.md §5.6 promotion contract, §8.3 drawing reliability table, §12
